matches.match_rationale: The core problem is paper records and legacy printouts at rural campuses that cannot be searched or retrieved quickly. OCR and form-field extraction directly converts these physical documents — enrollment registers, fee receipts, disciplinary logs — into machine-readable data that satisfies regulatory requests. This is the foundational digitization step without which nothing else is possible.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A document digitization service that ingests scanned or photographed paper records from each campus, extracts structured fields (student ID, date, record type, amounts), and indexes them into a searchable compliance archive ready for regulatory export.

matches.match_rationale: Many campuses have semi-structured legacy digital records — spreadsheets, CSV exports from old SIS software, inconsistently formatted databases — that exist but cannot be mapped to the standardized schema required by regulatory filings. AI-assisted schema mapping and normalization converts these heterogeneous sources into a unified, audit-ready format without manual re-entry. This directly addresses the 'cannot quickly verify' half of the pain point.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An ETL pipeline that accepts legacy exports in varied formats from each campus, uses LLM-assisted field mapping to normalize them to a standard compliance schema, and flags records with missing mandatory fields before submission deadlines.

matches.match_rationale: Once records are digitized, compliance officers need to rapidly answer specific regulatory questions — 'What was the enrollment count at Campus X in Term 2, 2021?' or 'List all disciplinary incidents involving fee disputes in 2022' — without manually browsing a file archive. RAG grounds answers in the actual indexed documents and provides citations, which is critical for audit defensibility. Nothing adequate currently exists for education operators at this scale.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A compliance Q&A interface where staff type regulatory questions in natural language and receive cited, document-grounded answers pulled from the multi-campus record archive, with source documents linked for regulator review.

matches.match_rationale: Responding to a regulatory filing request involves multiple sequential steps: identify which campuses and date ranges are in scope, locate and digitize relevant records, check for completeness gaps, assemble the required document package, and submit or flag for human review. This multi-step workflow with branching logic (missing records → trigger reconstruction process) is exactly what task automation agents address. The penalty cost of missed steps makes automation highly valuable.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An audit-response agent that, upon receiving a regulatory data request, autonomously scopes the required records across all campuses, identifies gaps, initiates digitization jobs for missing paper records, assembles the filing package, and routes to a compliance officer for sign-off.

matches.match_rationale: After digitization, multi-site operators face thousands of unclassified documents with inconsistent naming conventions across campuses. Text classification assigns each document to the correct regulatory category (enrollment record, fee schedule, disciplinary record, staff file) so that when a specific record type is requested by regulators it can be retrieved in seconds rather than hours. Without accurate classification, a searchable archive still fails at retrieval time.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An automated document tagger that classifies incoming digitized records by type, campus, academic year, and regulatory category, populating a structured index that drives the compliance search and reporting layer.

matches.match_rationale: Operators with 10+ campuses cannot manually audit every site's record completeness before filing season. A risk scoring model trained on historical audit flags, record age, campus type (rural vs urban), and last digitization date can surface which campuses are most likely to generate incomplete submissions, enabling proactive remediation rather than reactive scrambling. This shifts compliance posture from firefighting to prevention.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A campus-level compliance risk dashboard that scores each site monthly on record completeness likelihood, prioritizes remediation effort, and triggers automated digitization workflows for high-risk campuses before filing deadlines.
